preflight.label_discipline: pass — bold labels: 'Unacceptable' (1w), 'High risk' (2w), 'Limited risk' (2w), 'Minimal risk' (2w), all <= 4 words
preflight.concept_fidelity: pass — all four tiers + responses + examples trace to corpus 5.6 table and sections 5.2-5.5
preflight.scope_discipline: pass — no NIST / Executive Order / India MEITY nodes, no article-number nodes; only this topic's four tiers
preflight.title_discipline: pass — governing pyramid/population idea lives in the title banner, not a ghost node
preflight.single_path_discipline: pass — one straight TB path T1 --> T2 --> T3 --> T4

findings.detail: Four tiers read top-down in correct risk order (Unacceptable -> High -> Limited -> Minimal). Each box shows tier + law's response + one example, all legible. Risk-coded colors red(apex) -> orange -> yellow -> green(base) reinforce the hierarchy. Title banner plus 'very few systems' (apex) and 'most systems live here' (base) annotations convey the pyramid population gradient. No overlaps, no truncation, no unreadable text.

RESOLVED. Cycle 1 used numeric HTML entities (▲ — ▼) and   padding to force pyramid width; htmlLabels rendered the entities literally ('&▲', '&—', '▼') and the nbsp padding wrapped bold tier names onto two lines. Cycle 2 dropped entities/padding, used progressively longer plain subtitle text to widen boxes downward, and plain-word population notes. Re-rendered clean.
